intro: Leucanthemum zawadzkii, commonly known as Zawadzki's daisy or Hungarian daisy, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum zawadzkii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leucanthemum zawadzkii is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin disorders.
